result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would **make empathy the universal first language** — a force so woven into the fabric of society that every choice, from personal interactions to global policy, would be rooted in understanding the lived reality of others.

Not as a vague ideal, but as a tangible shift — where listening would come before judging, where systems would be designed to lift rather than exclude, and where difference would be met with curiosity instead of fear.  

It wouldn’t erase all problems, but it would dissolve the walls that keep us from solving them together.
